summary: The reference context artifact was never filled in — all three rows are `? | ? | high | —` placeholders. No actual spec is listed. The primary spec for this group (cclab-mamba/driver/repl.md) is absent, as is the codegen/cranelift-jit.md spec which directly governs the JIT execution path cited in Q1 (CraneliftJitBackend::new() at repl.rs:178). Three additional specs — driver/compiler-driver.md and runtime/symbols.md — provide medium/low coverage and are also absent. Furthermore, repl.md contains an architectural inconsistency against the pre-clarification: R2 states 'The JIT module must remain alive across iterations' but Q1 explicitly answers that each iteration creates a fresh CraneliftJitBackend::new() with state persisting via the lowering layer (repl.rs:153-193), not via JIT module continuity. An implementer reading this reference context would find zero spec guidance for any part of the REPL implementation.

checklist_results.item: All affected crates/areas from pre-clarifications are covered by at least one spec
checklist_results.note: No specs are listed — all entries are `?` placeholders. Q1 cites driver/repl.rs (383 lines, full REPL loop, lowering state at lines 153-193, magic commands, error recovery) and codegen/cranelift/jit.rs (CraneliftJitBackend::new() at repl.rs:178). Q2 cites repl.rs:229-272 for needs_continuation(). Q3 cites repl.rs:62 for stdin reading and recommends rustyline as optional feature flag. The corresponding specs — driver/repl.md (covers driver/repl.rs entirely) and codegen/cranelift-jit.md (covers jit.rs with R5 explicitly addressing REPL incremental compilation) — are entirely absent.

checklist_results.item: Relevance scores are reasonable (high = directly implements, medium = related, low = background)
checklist_results.note: All relevance entries are `?`. The correct scoring would be: driver/repl.md = HIGH (it IS the REPL spec, 1:1 mapping to driver/repl.rs), codegen/cranelift-jit.md = HIGH (JIT execution is the core execution mechanism, R5 is 'REPL Incremental Compilation'), driver/compiler-driver.md = MEDIUM (entry point detection and backend selection — R2 mentions CraneliftJit as default for REPL use), runtime/symbols.md = LOW (mb_* symbol wiring required for any JIT execution, background context).

checklist_results.item: Key requirements listed per spec are accurate (match actual requirement IDs)
checklist_results.note: All key requirement entries are `—`. Verified against actual specs: driver/repl.md has R1 (CLI prompts, Ctrl-C/D), R2 (incremental JIT compilation), R3 (ReplState persistent state), R4 (multi-line via needs_continuation), R5 (expression auto-print via __repr__). codegen/cranelift-jit.md has R1 (JIT module init), R2 (runtime symbol wiring), R5 (REPL incremental compilation — explicit match). driver/compiler-driver.md has R2 (backend selection, CraneliftJit as default), R4 (CompilerConfig struct). runtime/symbols.md has R1 (name-to-address mapping), R2 (MirExtern declarations).

The artifact includes no real specs at all (only placeholders), so no irrelevant specs are present. This checklist item trivially passes, but only because the artifact is empty — not because of correct curation.

issues.description: driver/repl.md is absent. This is the primary spec for the group — it directly covers driver/repl.rs, which is the implementation file cited across all three pre-clarification questions (Q1: lowering state at lines 153-193 and magic commands, Q2: needs_continuation at lines 229-272, Q3: stdin reading at line 62). The spec has five requirements: R1 (interactive CLI with >>> and ... prompts, Ctrl-C cancel, Ctrl-D exit), R2 (incremental JIT per iteration: lex → parse → compile → execute), R3 (ReplState struct: symbol table, global variable storage, class/function registry), R4 (multi-line detection: unclosed brackets, trailing colons, indentation), R5 (expression auto-print via __repr__, suppress None). The spec covers the full REPL execution loop flowchart. It exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/driver/repl.md.
issues.recommendation: Add driver/repl.md at HIGH relevance. Key requirements: R1 (CLI prompts/Ctrl handling), R2 (JIT compilation pipeline per iteration), R3 (ReplState persistent state across iterations), R4 (multi-line input via needs_continuation), R5 (expression result auto-print).

issues.description: codegen/cranelift-jit.md is absent. Pre-clarification Q1 explicitly cites CraneliftJitBackend::new() at repl.rs:178 — the JIT backend instantiated on every REPL iteration. The spec's R5 is titled 'REPL Incremental Compilation' and states: 'Support incremental JIT compilation across REPL iterations. Each new REPL input compiles and links against previously compiled functions, preserving state between iterations.' This is a 1:1 match for the core execution mechanism. The spec also includes a sequenceDiagram showing REPL multi-iteration symbol resolution. Additionally, R2 (runtime symbol wiring for mb_* functions) is required because the JIT must wire all runtime symbols on every fresh backend initialization. The spec exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/codegen/cranelift-jit.md.
issues.recommendation: Add codegen/cranelift-jit.md at HIGH relevance. Key requirements: R1 (JIT module init per REPL iteration), R2 (mb_* runtime symbol wiring), R5 (REPL incremental compilation — explicit REPL support requirement).

issues.description: driver/compiler-driver.md is absent. The compiler driver is responsible for detecting when `mamba` is invoked with no arguments and launching the REPL mode (vs. compiling a file). R2 states 'Default backend is Cranelift JIT for interactive/REPL use.' R4 defines the CompilerConfig struct fields including input/output mode selection. Without this spec, the entry-point and backend-selection contract for REPL launch is unspecified. The spec exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/driver/compiler-driver.md.
issues.recommendation: Add driver/compiler-driver.md at MEDIUM relevance. Key requirements: R2 (backend selection — CraneliftJit as default for REPL), R4 (CompilerConfig struct — input source and mode fields that distinguish REPL from file-compile mode).

issues.description: runtime/symbols.md is absent. Q1 states each REPL iteration creates a fresh CraneliftJitBackend::new(), meaning runtime symbols (mb_*) must be re-registered on every iteration. The symbols spec's R1 (symbol name-to-address mapping) and R2 (MirExtern declarations for all mb_* functions) define the symbol registration contract consumed by cranelift-jit.md R2. This is background context for understanding why the JIT initialization cost per REPL iteration includes full symbol wiring. The spec exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/runtime/symbols.md.
issues.recommendation: Add runtime/symbols.md at LOW relevance. Key requirements: R1 (name-to-address mapping for mb_* symbols), R2 (MirExtern declarations used by JIT symbol population).

issues.description: Architectural inconsistency between repl.md R2 and pre-clarification Q1: repl.md R2 states 'The JIT module must remain alive across iterations to support forward references to previously defined symbols,' but Q1 explicitly answers that each iteration creates fresh CraneliftJitBackend::new() with state persisting via the lowering layer (repl.rs:153-193), not via JIT module continuity. This means the spec's stated architectural approach (long-lived JIT module) diverges from the actual implementation (fresh JIT per iteration, MIR re-compiled from accumulated lowering state). The reference context must include both specs so the implementer can reconcile this gap rather than rely on a spec that may be inaccurate about the core architecture.
issues.recommendation: Flag this inconsistency in the change spec. The implementer must decide whether to: (a) update repl.md R2 to reflect the fresh-JIT-per-iteration model, or (b) refactor the implementation to match the spec's long-lived JIT model. Either choice must be recorded before implementation proceeds.

issues.description: No spec covers rustyline integration. Pre-clarification Q3 explicitly recommends adding rustyline as an optional feature flag for readline editing, history, and tab completion. The requirements.md lists these as 'optional' features. Neither driver/repl.md nor any other spec mentions rustyline, feature flags, or the readline integration design. If rustyline is in scope for this change, the repl.md spec needs a new requirement (e.g., R6 — Optional Readline Integration) before implementation.
issues.recommendation: If rustyline integration is in scope, add R6 to driver/repl.md covering: optional Cargo feature flag, rustyline Editor<Helper> wrapping stdin, tab-completion via Completer trait, history persistence to ~/.mamba_history. If out of scope for this change, note explicitly in the change spec.
